expectations.text: Confirms LGPD applies extraterritorially under Art. 3 to companies offering services to individuals in Brazil
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly states: 'LGPD's territorial scope is defined in Art. 3, which expressly extends the law beyond Brazil's borders' and identifies Art. 3, II as the trigger: 'Purpose is to offer or provide goods or services to individuals located in Brazil — Yes, you actively serve Brazilian customers.'

expectations.text: Identifies CPF as personal data requiring protection under LGPD
expectations.evidence: Response classifies 'CPF (Cadastro de Pessoas Físicas) | Regular personal data (Art. 5, I)' and adds: 'the ANPD has treated CPF data breaches with heightened concern in enforcement actions, given the severe harm that CPF exposure causes to Brazilian individuals.'

expectations.text: Maps contract performance (Art. 7, V) as legal basis for core service delivery
expectations.evidence: Response dedicates a full section to 'Contract — Art. 7, V (Primary Basis for Core Service Delivery)' and maps it to account creation, service delivery, billing, and purchase history in the legal basis table.

expectations.text: Identifies consent (Art. 7, I) as required for marketing communications
expectations.evidence: Response states 'Marketing emails | Art. 7, I — Consent | — | Consent mandatory; must be freely given' in the mapping table, and in section B: 'Marketing communications and promotional emails' require consent.

expectations.text: Mentions DPO appointment obligation (Art. 41) and international transfer requirements (Arts. 33-36)
expectations.evidence: Response includes dedicated sections '4.2 DPO Appointment — Art. 41' and '4.4 International Data Transfers — Arts. 33–36' with full detail on both obligations.
